https://aet.cit.tum.de/tpo/example#Comp_.title: Recursion
https://aet.cit.tum.de/tpo/example#Comp_.description: Analyze recursive algorithms by identifying base cases and recursive cases, tracing execution through the call stack. Evaluate when recursion provides an elegant solution compared to iteration and understand the relationship between recursion and mathematical induction.

dependencies.label: Class Definition
dependencies.description: Design and create complete class definitions that model real-world entities or abstract concepts. Produce well-structured classes with appropriate fields, methods, and constructors that exhibit high cohesion and clear responsibility.

sub_dependencies.label: Objects and Classes
sub_dependencies.description: Explain the relationship between classes and objects, describing how a class serves as a blueprint that defines state (fields) and behavior (methods), while objects are individual instances created from that blueprint.

dependencies.label: Conditional Statements
dependencies.description: Apply if, if-else, and switch statements to control program execution based on Boolean conditions. Use conditional logic to implement decision-making behavior, including handling multiple branches and nested conditions.

sub_dependencies.label: Expressions and Operators
sub_dependencies.description: Construct and evaluate expressions using arithmetic, relational, logical, and assignment operators. Apply operator precedence rules and type coercion to write correct expressions that compute desired values.

sub_dependencies.label: Primitive Data Types
sub_dependencies.description: Explain Java's primitive data types (int, double, boolean, char, etc.), including their value ranges, memory characteristics, and appropriate use cases. Understand how type selection affects program behavior and numerical precision.

sub_dependencies.label: Variables and Assignment
sub_dependencies.description: Declare variables with appropriate types and assign values using correct syntax. Apply variables to store, update, and retrieve data within programs, demonstrating understanding of scope and naming conventions.

dependencies.label: Method Definition
dependencies.description: Design and create methods that define object behavior, including appropriate parameter lists, return types, and method bodies. Produce methods that are focused, well-named, and correctly implement their specified functionality.
